utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , ms. hill . nice to see you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hi , dr. james , good to see you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , i'm seeing ms. hill . she's a 41-year-old female , and what brings you in today ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , i am having a lot of pain at the end of my right middle finger .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: what did you do ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: a little embarrassing . um , i got rear-ended , slow motor , uh , vehicle accident , and i got really angry with the person who hit me , so i went to flip him the bird , but i was a little too enthusiastic .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and i hit the ceiling of the car .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . when did this happen ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , it was saturday , so about four , five days ago .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: five days ago . what were you doing ? were you , like , stopped at a stoplight ? a stop sign ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes . so i was stopped at a four-way stop , and it was not my turn to go . there were other cars going , and the person behind me just was n't watching . i think they were texting and rear-ended me .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: how much damage to your car ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , not too much . the , the trunk crumpled up a little bit .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and no other injuries ? just the finger ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: just the middle finger .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so you would've escaped this accident without any injuries ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes . uh , i'm not proud .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . um , so four days of right middle finger pain-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . after a motor vehicle accident .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . um , let's look at your x-ray . hey , dragon , show me the last x-ray . so what i'm seeing here is on the tip of this middle finger , you actually have a fracture . so you have a distal phalanx fracture in the middle finger . very ...

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: great .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: very interesting . let me check it out . um , so does it hurt when i push right here ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and does that hurt ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: very much so .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: what about down here ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so generally , your exam is normal other than you've got tenderness over your distal phalanx of your right middle finger . um , so your diagnosis is distal phalanx fracture of the middle finger or the third finger . and i'm gon na put you on a little bit of pain medicine just to help , just , like , two days' worth . okay , so tramadol , 50 milligrams , every six hours as needed for pain . i'm gon na dispense eight of those .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and then , um , i'm gon na put you in a finger splint and have you come back in two weeks to get a follow-up x-ray . any questions for me ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes . so i'm taking digoxin for afib . will the tramadol be okay with that ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: it will be okay . so you have atrial fibrillation .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you take digoxin . all right . any other questions for me ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , that's it . thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you're welcome . hey , dragon , go ahead and finalize the recording , and , uh , come with me . we'll get you checked out .

detail_list.summary: The patient reports pain at the distal end of the right middle finger.
The doctor asks for the cause of the finger injury.
The patient sustained the injury after being rear-ended in a motor vehicle accident and striking the car ceiling.
The doctor asks for the duration of the injury.
The injury occurred five days ago.
The doctor asks if there are any other injuries.
The patient denies any other injuries.
X-ray imaging shows a comminuted distal phalanx fracture of the right middle finger.
The doctor asks if the patient experiences pain upon palpation of the distal phalanx.
The patient confirms tenderness over the distal phalanx.
Tramadol 50 mg every 6 hours as needed for pain, dispense 8.
The patient will be provided a finger splint and return in 2 weeks for a follow-up x-ray.
The patient asks about potential interactions between Tramadol and Digoxin for atrial fibrillation.
The doctor confirms the safety of the medication and acknowledges the patient's history of atrial fibrillation.

case_description: right middle finger pain; atrial fibrillation
